attack_chain.description: Attacker created and immediately deleted branches in the IaC repo with a large number of file changes to hide their activity while triggering CI/CD workflows.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The malicious actor created and immediately deleted branches to trigger CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Deployment) workflows in our IaC (infrastructure as code) repository. The branches changed a very large number of files to remain hidden.

evidence_chunks.quote: The malicious actor created and immediately deleted branches to trigger CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Deployment) workflows in our IaC (infrastructure as code) repository. The branches changed a very large number of files to remain hidden.

The malicious actor created and immediately deleted branches to trigger CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Deployment) workflows in our IaC (infrastructure as code) repository. The branches changed a very large number of files to remain hidden.

attack_chain.description: From the triggered CI workflows, the attacker harvested stored secrets and cloud credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: From the triggered CI workflows, they harvested stored secrets and cloud credentials from these workflows.

evidence_chunks.quote: From the triggered CI workflows, they harvested stored secrets and cloud credentials from these workflows.
From the triggered CI workflows, they harvested stored secrets and cloud credentials from these workflows.

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
attack_chain.description: Used harvested credentials to access cloud service accounts and production systems across AWS, GCP, and bare-metal infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: These credentials granted access to cloud service accounts and production systems across Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and our bare-metal infrastructure.

evidence_chunks.quote: These credentials granted access to cloud service accounts and production systems across Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and our bare-metal infrastructure.

attack_chain.evidence_quote: these commands were launched from thousands of different IP addresses

evidence_chunks.quote: these commands were launched from thousands of different IP addresses
these commands were launched from thousands of different IP addresses

attack_chain.description: Injected a malicious payload into a running Kubernetes pod hosting the Kiln Connect API, modifying endpoint logic at runtime without altering images or code repos.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor injected a malicious payload into a running Kubernetes pod hosting the Kiln Connect API, modifying the logic of a Kiln API endpoint

evidence_chunks.quote: The threat actor injected a malicious payload into a running Kubernetes pod hosting the Kiln Connect API, modifying the logic of a Kiln API endpoint
The threat actor injected a malicious payload into a running Kubernetes pod hosting the Kiln Connect API, modifying the logic of a Kiln API endpoint

attack_chain.description: Modified the API endpoint to return a malicious Solana transaction that changed the withdrawal authority when the stake account held more than 150k SOL.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: modifying the logic of a Kiln API endpoint to return a malicious transaction... The malicious transaction changed the withdrawal authority of the Solana stakes, only if the existing withdrawal authority of the stake account provided in the POST call held stake balances above 150k SOL.

attack_chain.description: A Kiln enterprise customer unknowingly signed the malicious transaction in their custody solution on Aug 31, transferring withdrawal authority over SOL stake accounts to the attacker, causing loss of funds.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: One Kiln client was directly impacted when they used Kiln Dashboard to unstake one of their SOL stake accounts on August 31st. The malicious transaction was forwarded from Kiln Dashboard to their custody solution, where it was approved by a quorum of signatories

evidence_chunks.quote: One Kiln client was directly impacted when they used Kiln Dashboard to unstake one of their SOL stake accounts on August 31st. The malicious transaction was forwarded from Kiln Dashboard to their custody solution, where it was approved by a quorum of signatories

One Kiln client was directly impacted when they used Kiln Dashboard to unstake one of their SOL stake accounts on August 31st. The malicious transaction was forwarded from Kiln Dashboard to their custody solution, where it was approved by a quorum of signatories

attack_chain.description: Attacker avoided persistence artifacts — no files written, no repo or container image modifications, operating only inside short-lived workloads.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor avoided leaving traces by never writing persistent files, never modifying code in repositories, and never modifying container images or databases.

evidence_chunks.quote: The threat actor avoided leaving traces by never writing persistent files, never modifying code in repositories, and never modifying container images or databases.
The threat actor avoided leaving traces by never writing persistent files, never modifying code in repositories, and never modifying container images or databases.

initial_access_vector: Compromise of a GitHub access token belonging to a Kiln infrastructure engineer (exact compromise method unknown).
persistence_mechanism: None persistent on Kiln infrastructure — attacker operated in-memory within short-lived cloud workloads. Persistence of impact achieved by transferring Solana stake account withdrawal authority to attacker-controlled address.
privilege_escalation: Harvested cloud credentials and secrets from CI/CD workflow environments to gain access to AWS, GCP, and Kubernetes production systems.
